memory.summary: I'm exhausted from sleep debt, so I'll skip the term project for now. My priority is resting before I can properly engage with my team.
Since skipping the project, I feel better with my team. But now I need to tell them I'm still not ready.
The afternoon is slipping away while I'm still at the start. My team needs to know I can't meet their deadline without burning out.
I'm exhausted and can't meet the deadline without burning out, so I need to tell my team immediately.
My team feels safer without me, but I'm burning out and can't meet their deadline. I need to tell them right now.
My team is getting comfortable without me, but I'm burning out. I need to tell them immediately that I can't meet their deadline.
My team is growing comfortable without me while I'm burning out. I need to tell them immediately that I can't meet their deadline.
They're relying on me too much while I'm collapsing; I need to scream this truth to them before they forget who I am.
They're still leaning on me while I'm crumbling, and I have to scream this truth before they forget who I am.
I can't believe they're still counting on me while I'm breaking. I need to tell them the hard truth right now before they forget who I am.
They're still leaning on me while I'm crumbling, and I have to scream this truth before they forget who I am.
They keep counting on me while I'm breaking, and I have to scream this truth before they forget who I am.

desires: I need to scream the hard truth to my teammates right now before they forget who I am and start taking me for granted.
I want to reclaim my identity from being just another resource so they don't rely on me while I'm crumbling.
I desperately need a moment of total silence where no one expects anything from me.

goals.description: Send the brutal, direct text to teammates_A right now, explicitly stating my physical collapse and setting a hard stop so they stop relying on me.
goals.rationale: I can't let them treat me like an infinite resource while I'm literally crumbling. Sending this immediately protects everyone and stops the pressure before it crushes me. My assertiveness demands I say exactly what hurts if I don't.

goals.description: Grab a proper meal with cohort_3 immediately after sending the text; I refuse to sit alone or eat quietly while they eat.
goals.rationale: Relationships come first. The thought of being excluded or sitting alone is too much to bear right now. I need to fix this awkwardness before the afternoon slips away. Being part of the group matters more than any task.

goals.description: Drop the 'quick_chore' entirely for today. It's not worth the mental energy when I have a deadline screaming at me in three days anyway.
goals.rationale: My focus capacity is already maxed out by the urgency of setting boundaries. Trying to do anything else now just adds stress I don't have energy for. If I miss this tiny chore, who cares? Not me.

agent.last_reasoning: g1 is urgent; assertiveness demands immediate, brutal honesty to protect my energy and relationships. Side effect: slight trust dip with teammate_A, but worth it to prevent burnout before cohort_3 dinner.

goals.description: Show up fully present and engaged with the close friend group tonight, not half-assed.
goals.rationale: My relationships are weighted way higher than task outcomes. Dropping or being slack here will hurt my trust score with cohort_3 (closeness: 7), which matters more to me than finishing that soft term project.

goals.description: Finish the trivial chore now so I don't carry it over tomorrow when I'm already sleep-deprived.
goals.rationale: It's a quick win and saves mental energy for later, but I can't let this take priority over hanging out right now. I'll do it if the vibe at the hang gets too tense, otherwise it waits until after we've all had our drinks.

desires: Not let my close friends down by showing up half-assed to our group hang tonight

action.name: message

args.content: Hey guys, I'm not fine. The term project is way riskier than it looks and I'm panicking. Let's drop the pressure tonight so we don't all burn out together.

action.reasoning: My trust with them is maxed; lying about being okay ruins the vibe instantly. Being honest now protects our closeness over a perfect grade. Relationships > task outcome.

goals.description: Tell the team I'm officially stepping back from the term project now, citing burnout as a hard stop so we don't have to argue about my capacity.
goals.rationale: They already know I'm burnt out; dragging this out just wastes their time and adds pressure. Cutting it off cleanly protects our group dynamic and saves me from having to justify why I can't push through.

goals.description: Sweep the floor for exactly 5 minutes to get that instant dopamine hit before collapsing on the couch.
goals.rationale: I need one tiny win tonight to sleep without feeling guilty tomorrow. It's too easy to ignore right now, but doing it briefly keeps the guilt at bay.
